name: I think my lung problems are from chemicals at work in my Murray factory job, how do I file for occupational illness workers comp?
description: Report to your supervisor ASAP, file Form 122 with Utah Labor Commission, and get medical proof linking illness to work exposure.[1]

mainEntity.name: I think my lung problems are from chemicals at work in my Murray factory job, how do I file for occupational illness workers comp?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report to your supervisor ASAP, file Form 122 with Utah Labor Commission, and get medical proof linking illness to work exposure.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: File a workers' compensation claim by reporting to your supervisor immediately and submitting Form 122 to Utah's Industrial Accidents Division.[1] Utah covers occupational illnesses like chemical-exposure lung disease if they arise out of and in the course of employment (AOE/COE).[1] Key factors: Provide medical evidence linking the illness to work; report within 180 days or risk denial; get treatment from authorized providers.[1] Document everything in writing, follow medical advice strictly, and consider consulting a workers' comp attorney if denied. Early action preserves your benefits for medical care and lost wages.
